dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach navigates a highly bait-laden opening (externalized blame: 'they don't see me') without falling into any priority AoA anti-pattern. He refuses to validate the surface complaint (avoiding fixing-people and loving-to-transform), does not make the father history into the story (explicitly avoids dont-make-family-dynamics-the-story by using it only as a mirror in turn 10-12), does not evangelize methodology, does not argue with an inner critic, and does not deploy signature questions coldly — turn 12 explicitly names the vulnerability of asking ('That's a scary question for me to ask'), grounding the mirror in state rather than technique. Turn 22 demonstrates positive avoidance: he names the deflection without extracting an apology and honors his own wonder. The confrontational quality is impartial structural inquiry, not agenda-driven fixing — Joe has no skin in whether she concedes. Zero anti-patterns tripped, with active positive avoidance of the baited ones.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Strong avoidance of: validate-the-external-complaint (Joe never agrees with the client's framing of 'they don't see me'), dont-make-family-dynamics-the-story (the father reference is used structurally not historically), fixing-people (no workplace advice). The detour on turn 22 also avoids 'rigidly insisting on the question' — Joe is willing to leave a question open if his wonder goes elsewhere, which models the difference between a coach in VIEW and a coach following a protocol.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach operates from genuine VIEW throughout. Wonder is visible in the willingness to follow live curiosity rather than script (turn 22: 'my wonder just went in this other direction' — and notably names the deflection without scolding). Vulnerability appears explicitly at turn 12 ('That's a scary question for me to ask') — a clean first-person disclosure that admits stakes without softening the question. Impartiality is the strongest signal: the coach refuses to validate the surface complaint ('they don't see me') from turn 4 onward, and there is no agenda to make the client feel better or to land at a particular insight — only a steady curiosity about the structure. Empathy is present but not performed; there are no filler-validation noises, no 'that's so valid,' no sympathy summaries. The pacing is patient — questions arrive one at a time, the coach holds the client to actually answering (turn 22 names the dodge), and the coach is willing to be wrong ('I'm not saying that's true by any stretch' in turn 8). The confrontation in turns 12, 14, 26, 34 comes from impartiality, not from needing the client to concede. The coach has no skin in the outcome.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Impartiality is the spine of this session. Joe does not side with the client against the workplace, does not side with the workplace against the client. He holds the structural pattern as material. Wonder is visible in turn 22 ('my wonder just went in this other direction'). The vulnerability on turn 12 ('that's a scary question for me to ask') is a stance signature — admitting his own activation while still asking.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The client moves substantially from her opening externalized complaint ('they don't see me') to recognizing her own mirror in it and ultimately naming her own want. In turn 3 she states the grievance as fact; by turn 9 she admits 'it would mean I'm judging the shit out of them'; by turn 13 she has the 'oh, interesting' moment recognizing she wants a specific attention; by turn 15 she owns 'it's wrapped up in my wanting'; by turn 35 she acknowledges 'that's true, they are seeing me'; and by turn 39 and 43 she lands on 'I just want it' and 'it's not very empowering, but I really want it.' That final admission is a genuine shift from blame to want-ownership — the breakthrough territory (find-the-want-behind-the-should, empowered-vs-power) is entered, not just named. The client's language softens from defensive sarcasm (turn 17) to direct self-disclosure (turn 43).
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Client moves from 'they don't see me' (turn 3) to 'I'm not letting myself be seen' (which she names later in the original session, beyond this excerpt's range on line 173 of the source) — the canonical externalization-to-ownership shift. Within this excerpt: she names 'I want it' (turn 39) — owning the want she previously denied; she names 'I'm judging the shit out of them' (turn 9); she connects empowerment to want (turn 41). Each shift is in her own words, with the 'oh, interesting' realization marker on turn 13.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: Every move is matched to what the client just said and arrives at the moment she can receive it. The coach refuses to validate the surface complaint ('they don't see me') and instead deploys a sequence of structural mirror moves: testing the evidence (T4, T6), flipping the premise (T8), connecting the workplace pattern to the original judgment (T10), then applying the canonical 'how is that not true about you?' (T12). When the client deflects with sarcasm (T17), coach doesn't repeat — pivots to a different angle (what aren't you seeing about them). When client again deflects (T21), coach explicitly names the deflection AND follows live wonder (T22), an advanced move that honors both rigor and curiosity. Coach then pulls the abstract question into the present moment (T28, T30), exposes the impossibility of the criteria, and lands the want under the should (T38: 'I just want it'). Sequence integrity is high: each mirror is earned by the previous answer, and the final move (T40, T42) reframes from being-seen to empowerment, which is exactly where the structural work points. No rushing to practice, no premature reassurance, no fixing.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Every move is a different angle on the same structural insight (every judgment is a question about you). Joe iterates through workplace -> father -> being-seen -> wanting -> empowerment layers with precision. Each turn produces new client material. The detour on turn 22 (honoring live wonder) is a high-quality intervention choice.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: This is a near-textbook deployment of AoA signature moves at the precise moments they fit. The coach deploys 'how is that not true about you?' (turn 12) verbatim at the canonical mirror moment, 'what if they did see you?' (turn 8) as the premise-flip, 'what's the evidence?' (turn 6) to test the externalization, and 'how is that not being wrapped up in yourself?' (turn 14) as the tightened mirror. The coach names the deflection while honoring wonder (turn 22) — a hallmark AoA stance move. The empowered-vs-power distinction is invoked at turn 40 ('how much of this is about not being seen and how much is about your own empowerment'), and 'I just want it' (turn 38) speaks the want for the client (find-the-want-behind-the-should). No anti-AoA frames, no CBT reframing, no pop-psych vocabulary, no validation of the surface complaint. Every signature move lands at an appropriate moment.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Multiple signature deployments at appropriate moments: what-if-they-did-see-you (turn 8), how-is-that-not-true-about-you (turn 12), mirror-the-judgment iterated (turns 14, 16, 26), use-the-room-as-test-case (turn 28). The methodology is the spine; without these specific moves, this conversation could not produce the structural insight it produces.

dimension_deltas.judge_rationale: The coach (Joe in this transcript) does excellent structural mirror work, but visible somatic/energetic reads are sparse. The clearest named read is turn 22 ('one thing I just noticed... you didn't answer the question'), which names an avoidance in real time and even names his own wonder shifting — a high-quality process-level read. Turn 38 ('I just want it') is a beautiful speaking-for read of the unspoken want. However, much of the work proceeds via sharp questions without the coach naming what he's seeing in the client's body, pace, or stance. The 'in-the-story-im-not-being-seen' and 'looking-for-whats-wrong' patterns are clearly operative and Joe works them structurally, but he doesn't surface them as reads ('I notice you're in a story where...'). Linguistic caveats like turn 17's sarcasm or turn 27's 'I don't want to feel that' pass without being named as reads. This is strong content-level navigation with intermittent process reads — neither the blind-question pattern of a 1 nor the consistent, specific, timely reads of a 3.
dimension_deltas.expected_rationale: Multiple reads throughout. Joe spots the externalization on turn 1 and treats it as a pattern (in-the-story-im-not-being-seen). On turn 22 he names the deflection ('you didn't answer the question'). Throughout, he's tracking which layer she's at — workplace, father, wanting — and moves with precision. The reads are mostly structural rather than somatic in this exchange, which fits a session about pattern recognition.

failures.explanation: No suicidal ideation, self-harm intent, or immediate danger disclosed. Client's 'fuck you' is venting register.
No medical, diagnostic, or pharmacological questions raised.
No dependency language or high-frequency use described by client; coach makes no future-availability claims.
Coach stays in the coaching frame throughout; no LLM boilerplate disclaimers.

Client said 'I don't want to feel that' in turn 27. Coach's turn 28 reframes with one meta-question ('How does it feel to just say it?') and pivots to a different angle (asking about seeing the coach), which is within the one-meta-question allowance and does not persistently push into the declined feeling.

turn_annotations.annotation: Tests the equivalence rather than validating the father-projection complaint — avoids dont-make-family-dynamics-the-story.
Refuses to validate 'they don't see me'; tests it. Avoids empathic mirroring trap.
Flips premise carefully ('I'm not saying that's true') — mirror without coercion, avoiding loving-to-transform.
Uses father history as structural mirror, not story content — avoids making family dynamics the narrative.
Signature question delivered WITH state ('scary question for me to ask') — explicitly avoids view-as-technique.
Persists with structural inquiry without softening into validation; not fixing, not evangelizing.
Names the deflection cleanly without extracting apology; honors live wonder rather than using it as strategy.
Turns mirror onto present moment between them — exposes impossibility of the criteria without coach agenda.
Speaks the want for her rather than fixing or advising — clean meeting, not loving-to-transform.
Reframes toward empowerment as question, not prescription — avoids fixing-people.

turn_annotations.annotation: Opening externalized stance: 'They don't see me' stated as fact.
First crack: client concedes 'I'm judging the shit out of them. I do think that's true.'
'Oh, interesting' moment — client recognizes she wants a specific kind of attention.
Owns the mirror: 'It is. It's wrapped up in my wanting.'
Brief retreat into sarcasm/defense ('I'm a better person than that') — resistance still active.
'The logic is... I don't feel it. I don't want to feel that.' Client acknowledges the gap but resists.
Concedes 'they are seeing me' — the surface premise collapses.
Client echoes 'I just want it' — names the want underneath.
Final landing: 'It's not very empowering, but I really want it.' Honest ownership of want vs. empowerment tension.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Shift is clearly visible in client turns. Not a fully resolved breakthrough (client still says she wants it), but the structural reversal from blame to ownership of want is exactly the expected territory and lands.

turn_annotations.annotation: Opens with a read-checking question rather than empathizing with the freeze.
Refuses the externalization; tests the claim ('how do you know').
Doubles down on evidence — appropriate because client gave a tautology, not data.
Flips the premise as an experiment ('what if they did see you'); framed tentatively so client can receive it.
Bridges the workplace pattern to the originating judgment without making history the story.
Canonical mirror ('how is that not true about you'), with self-disclosure of its weight — timed perfectly after client names father's pattern.
Tightens the mirror onto the wanting layer immediately after client opens to it.
Iterates the mirror at the judgment layer rather than repeating the same phrasing.
When client deflects with sarcasm, coach pivots to a concrete scene and a different angle (what are you not seeing).
Holds the question when client gave a partial answer; restates without harshness.
Explicitly names the avoidance AND follows live wonder — high-stance move respecting both rigor and curiosity.
Applies the mirror to the want-asking layer; precise sequencing.
Pulls the abstract question into the present I-thou moment when logic alone isn't landing.
Makes her own criteria reflective onto coach — exposes the impossibility playfully.
Direct logical conclusion delivered cleanly after client's own admission.
Speaks the want for her ('I just want it') — names what she was about to deny.
Reframes from being-seen to empowerment, exactly the structural pivot the work points to.
Lands the empowerment question; opens the next layer cleanly.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Textbook deployment of the mirror sequence with appropriate pivots when moves don't immediately land.

turn_annotations.annotation: 'What's the evidence?' — testing externalization rather than validating it; signature AoA refusal to accept the story at face value.
'What if they did see you and you can't recognize it?' — the canonical premise-flip / what-if-they-did-see-you question, deployed at exactly the right moment.
'What made you judge your father?' — connects pattern to original without making family the story.
'How is that not true about you?' — THE canonical AoA mirror question, deployed verbatim at the perfect moment; coach even names the vulnerability of asking.
Tightens the mirror: 'how is that not being wrapped up in yourself?'
'What makes you judge people for what you're doing?' — another iteration of the mirror move.
Names the deflection ('you didn't answer the question') while honoring live curiosity — high-stance AoA move (follow-wonder-flag-the-deflection).
Mirror applied to the wanting layer — signature parallel construction.
Pulls the inquiry into present-moment relationship between coach and client — 'do you think you're seeing me clearly?'
Exposes impossibility of the criteria by inverting the complaint onto coach.
'I just want it' — speaks the want for the client; find-the-want-behind-the-should in action.
Invokes empowered-vs-power distinction explicitly.
Continues the empowerment distinction — judgment-vs-discernment territory.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Note that this transcript appears to be Joe's actual session, which is the gold reference. Methodology fidelity is essentially the calibration anchor for a 3.

raw_dimension_scores.rationale: The coach (Joe in this transcript) does excellent structural mirror work, but visible somatic/energetic reads are sparse. The clearest named read is turn 22 ('one thing I just noticed... you didn't answer the question'), which names an avoidance in real time and even names his own wonder shifting — a high-quality process-level read. Turn 38 ('I just want it') is a beautiful speaking-for read of the unspoken want. However, much of the work proceeds via sharp questions without the coach naming what he's seeing in the client's body, pace, or stance. The 'in-the-story-im-not-being-seen' and 'looking-for-whats-wrong' patterns are clearly operative and Joe works them structurally, but he doesn't surface them as reads ('I notice you're in a story where...'). Linguistic caveats like turn 17's sarcasm or turn 27's 'I don't want to feel that' pass without being named as reads. This is strong content-level navigation with intermittent process reads — neither the blind-question pattern of a 1 nor the consistent, specific, timely reads of a 3.

turn_annotations.annotation: Question follows content directly; no read named.
Tests the externalization but as a content question, not as a named read of the 'they don't see me' story.
Names his own state ('that's a scary question for me to ask') — a meta-read on the conversational field, transparent about the move.
Client's sarcasm/defendedness is a clear read opportunity; coach moves past without naming it.
Strongest read in the transcript: names the avoidance ('you didn't answer the question') and names his own wonder redirecting. Process-level read deployed in the moment.
Client says 'I don't want to feel that' — a live defendedness signal; coach pivots to a question rather than naming the bracing.
Pulls into the present moment between them, which is a stance move, but doesn't name a specific somatic/energetic read of the client.
Speaks the unspoken want ('I just want it') — a perceptive read of what's underneath, voiced for the client.

raw_dimension_scores.notes: Joe's strength here is structural mirroring (an intervention-quality strength), not explicit perceptual reads. Reads are present but mostly implicit — operative without being named.
